_comment: Absolute CI bench ceilings (ADR-023). Deliberately generous — shared GitHub runners are noisy; this gate catches order-of-magnitude regressions, not single-digit-percent drift, which the reference-machine Criterion history tracks instead. Every entry records its derivation so recalibration is an evidence exercise. Recalibrated 2026-07-16 from Surajit's first full (non-quick) reference-machine run of the M3 bench suite — all ten entries are now measured, none analytical.

scene_graph::insert_shapes/10000.basis: 375.47 µs, 2026-07-16 reference run × ~7 CI margin. Up from the pre-M3 352 µs baseline — every insert now also pushes the new paint-order Vec (store()); the ~23 µs/10k delta is that cost, architecturally required for M3's order-exact undo.

scene_graph::render_list_all_visible/100.basis: 356.76 µs, 2026-07-16 reference run × ~7.3 CI margin. Up from 331.6 µs pre-M3 — get_render_list now indexes through the order Vec (an extra bounds-checked lookup per node) instead of iterating self.nodes directly.

scene_graph::hit_test_miss/1000.basis: MEASURED 1.8809 µs, 2026-07-16 reference run × ~21 CI margin. ADR-023 had extrapolated ~1.3 µs — same order of magnitude, now confirmed.

scene_graph::hit_test_miss/10000.basis: MEASURED 17.665 µs, 2026-07-16 reference run × ~17 CI margin. ADR-023 had extrapolated 13–30 µs — landed inside that range.

scene_graph::hit_test_miss/100000.basis: MEASURED 361.25 µs, 2026-07-16 reference run × ~7.2 CI margin. ADR-023 had extrapolated 0.3–0.6 ms — landed at the low end, well inside the <1ms system target.

scene_graph::hit_test_top_10k.basis: MEASURED 7.66 ns, 2026-07-16 reference run. Nanosecond-scale — the ceiling is a large ABSOLUTE margin (~2,600x) rather than a tight multiplier, since clock resolution and scheduler jitter dominate noise at this magnitude, not the operation itself.

scene_graph::move_node_to_index_10k.basis: MEASURED 114.16 µs, 2026-07-16 reference run × ~7 CI margin. Worst case: linear search (O(n) position()) + two O(n) shifts (remove + insert) on a 10k-element Vec — three passes, consistent with the measurement.
